_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-18 Responsibilities of MCA Participants (Revised 5/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-18: Responsibilities of MCA Participants — MDHHS Approved 5/30/2023
purpose: Define the responsibilities of all Medical Control Authority (MCA) participants including the MCA, participating EMS agencies, medical directors, and providers. MCAs are the administrative and medical oversight structure for prehospital EMS in Michigan. All EMS agencies operating within an MCA jurisdiction must comply with MCA requirements.

sections.title: MCA Responsibilities
points: Develop, review, and update EMS protocols for providers operating within MCA jurisdiction
Designate an MCA Medical Director who is responsible for clinical oversight
Establish a Quality Improvement (QI) program per Section 8-27
Establish a PSRO (Professional Standards Review Organization) per Section 8-29
Submit MCA protocol updates to MDHHS for review and approval
Ensure participating EMS agencies comply with MCA and state EMS requirements
Provide mechanism for online medical control available to all providers in the jurisdiction 24/7
Review and act on PSRO findings and complaints per Section 8-24 and 8-25

sections.title: MCA Medical Director Responsibilities
points: Serve as the responsible physician for all offline medical control in the MCA jurisdiction
Review, approve, and sign all MCA protocol selections and clinical policies
Oversee and participate in the QI/QA program
Be available for consultation by online medical control physicians
Investigate and respond to protocol deviations, complaints, and adverse events
Ensure continuing medical education opportunities are available for providers within the MCA

sections.title: EMS Agency Responsibilities
points: Employ and deploy only licensed EMS providers at appropriate licensure levels
Comply with all MCA protocols and selections
Submit ePCR data to Michigan EMSIS per Section 8-13
Participate in MCA QI and PSRO programs
Maintain equipment and medications to support authorized protocols
Provide initial and continuing education to providers
Notify MCA of: provider disciplinary actions, criminal charges/convictions per Section 8-26, protocol deviations, adverse events

sections.title: EMS Provider Responsibilities
points: Maintain current Michigan EMS licensure and all required certifications
Practice only within the scope defined by Michigan law, MCA protocols, and the provider's licensure level
Complete required continuing education
Complete accurate and timely PCRs per Section 7-16 and 8-13
Report protocol deviations, adverse events, and safety concerns to MCA/medical director
Cooperate with MCA QI and PSRO processes
Report criminal charges and convictions per Section 8-26
Maintain fitness for duty — report to supervisor if impaired or unable to perform duties safely

scope_notes: MCA: protocols, medical director, QI program, PSRO, 24/7 online MC, MDHHS reporting
MCA Medical Director: offline MC authority, protocol approval, QI oversight, adverse event response
EMS Agency: licensed providers, protocol compliance, EMSIS submission, QI participation, MCA notification of adverse events
EMS Provider: maintain licensure, practice within scope, complete PCRs, report deviations, cooperate with QI/PSRO
Providers must report criminal charges and convictions per Section 8-26
Fitness for duty: report impairment to supervisor
